evidence_excerpt: At the time of the scheduled follow-up, her condition had deteriorated to the point she was moved to a nursing home facility for people with dementia and could not be tested.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Progressive language decline with difficulties formulating and repeating sentences, phonemic and semantic paraphasias, and subsequent cognitive deterioration.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: She improved with each training set and reached criterion more quickly for successive sets; also showed significant improvement in pointing to named letters.
By the scheduled 6-month follow-up, her broader cognitive condition had deteriorated and she required nursing home care.

notes_on_uncertainty: The source describes two cases; this summary refers to the logopenic variant PPA patient (FSE). Exact dates are not provided; timing is inferred (therapy sessions approximately weekly; deterioration noted at the time of a planned 6‑month follow-up). Long-term functional and symptom assessments are based on documented nursing home placement and described progressive language/cognitive decline.
